description: Demonstrates a binary disjoint classes axiom based on example in the Structural Specification and Functional-Style Syntax document.
Demonstrates a binary disjoint classes axiom and class assertions causing an inconsistency based on example in the Structural Specification and Functional-Style Syntax document.
A modification of test DisjointClasses-001 to demonstrate a ternary disjoint classes axiom.
Functional syntax to RDFXML translation of ontology consisting of a 2 argument DifferentIndividuals
Functional syntax to RDFXML translation of ontology consisting of a 3 argument DifferentIndividuals
Functional syntax to RDFXML checking that there aren't builtin prefixes for xsd, rdf, rdfs, owl
Functional syntax to RDFXML translation of ontology consisting of a 2 argument SameIndividual
Demonstrates annotation of an annotation.  Adapted from an example in the Mapping to RDF Graphs document.
Demonstrates use of an asymmetric object property axiom to cause a trivial inconsistency based on the example in the Structural Specification and Functional-Style Syntax document.
Demonstrates axiom annotation based on an example in the Mapping to RDF Graphs document.  The axiom being annotated here generates a main triple when mapped to RDF.
Demonstrates use of a disjoint data properties axiom to create a trivial inconsistency based on the example in the Structural Specification and Functional-Style Syntax document.
Demonstrates use of a ternary disjoint data properties axiom to infer different individuals.  Adapted from test New-Feature-DisjointDataProperties-001.
Demonstrates use of a disjoint object properties axiom to infer that two individuals are different based on the example in the Structural Specification and Functional-Style Syntax document.
A modification of test New-Feature-DisjointObjectProperties-001 to demonstrate a ternary disjoint object properties axiom.
Demonstrates use of an irreflexive object property axiom to cause a trivial inconsistency based on the example in the Structural Specification and Functional-Style Syntax document.
Demonstrates use of a "localized" key axiom to merge individuals based on an example in the Structural Specification and Functional-Style Syntax document.

Demonstrates that use of a "localized" key axiom only merges individuals that are instances of the given class expression.  Based on an example in the Structural Specification and Functional-Style Syntax document.
Demonstrates that a key axiom does not make all properties used in it functional. Based on an example in the Structural Specification and Functional-Style Syntax document.

Demonstrates that a key axiom does not make all properties used in it functional, but these properties may be made functional with other axioms. Based on an example in the Structural Specification and Functional-Style Syntax document.
Demonstrates use of a negative data property assertion to create a trivial inconsistency based on an example in the Structural Specification and Functional-Style Syntax document.
Demonstrates use of a negative object property assertion to create a trivial inconsistency based on an example in the Structural Specification and Functional-Style Syntax document.
Demonstrates an object property chain in a subproperty axiom based on the example in the Structural Specification and Functional-Style Syntax document.
A simple test of role chains and role hierarchy.
A test of an interaction between a role chain +hierarchy and transitivity axioms.
Demonstrates a qualified maximum cardinality object property restriction adapted from example in the Structural Specification and Functional-Style Syntax document.
Uses a reflexive object property axiom to infer a property value based on the example in the Structural Specification and Functional-Style Syntax document.

id: Plus and Minus Zero are Distinct
description: For floats and double, +0.0 and -0.0 are distinct values, which contradicts the functionality for numberOfChildren.
URI references used in annotations don't need to be typed.
AnnotationProperty's in OWL Lite and OWL DL, may not have range constraints.  They are permitted in OWL 2 DL.
owl:sameAs is stronger than owl:equivalentClass.
Under the direct semantics, test WebOnt-I4.6-005 must be treated as a positive entailment test because the direct semantics ignore annotations in the conclusion ontology.

Structure sharing was not permitted in OWL DL, between a class description
and a type triple, but is permitted in OWL 2 DL.

Structure sharing was not permitted in OWL DL, between an
owl:equivalentClass triple
and a type triple, but is permitted in OWL 2 DL.

Structure sharing was not permitted in OWL DL, between an owl:equivalentClass triple and an
owl:disjointWith triple, but is permitted in OWL 2 DL.

The abstract syntax form of the conclusions is: EquivalentClasses( restriction( first:p, minCardinality(1) ) ) ObjectProperty( first:p ) This is trivially true given that first:p is an individualvaluedPropertyID.
This test exhibits the effect of the comprehension principles in OWL Full.  The conclusion ontology only contains a class declaration, ObjectUnionOf class expression does not appear in an axiom.
All xsd:byte are xsd:short.
-1 is an xsd:short that is not an xsd:unsignedByte.
-1 is an xsd:short that is not an xsd:unsignedShort; 100000 is an xsd:unsignedInt that is not an xsd:unsignedShort; but there are no xsd:unsignedShort which are neither xsd:short nor xsd:unsignedInt
0 is the only xsd:nonNegativeInteger which is also an xsd:nonPositiveInteger. 0 is an xsd:short.
The empty graph entails that xsd:integer and xsd:string are a rdfs:Datatype
The triple asserts something of type owl:Nothing, however that is the empty class.

In OWL Lite and DL the subject and object of a triple with predicate owl:backwardCompatibleWith must both be explicitly typed as owl:Ontology. In OWL 2, this RDF graph parses to a single ontology with URI http://www.example.org/ and an annotation assertion between a blank node and that URI.

DL Test: heinsohn1.1
Tbox tests from Heinsohn et al.

Tests incoherency caused by disjoint concept

DL Test: heinsohn1.3
Tbox tests from Heinsohn et al.

Tests incoherency caused by disjoint concept

DL Test: heinsohn1.4
Tbox tests from Heinsohn et al.

Tests incoherency caused by disjoint concept
differentFrom is a SymmetricProperty.

If the owl:disjointWith edges in the graph form an undirected complete subgraph 
then this may be within OWL DL.

This example has owl:disjointWith edges in the graph which cannot be generated
by the mapping rules for DisjointClasses. Consider the lack of owl:disjointWith edge
between nodes C and D.

If the owl:disjointWith edges in the graph form unconnected
undirected complete subgraphs
then this may be within OWL DL.

If the owl:disjointWith edges in the graph form
undirected complete subgraphs which share blank nodes
then this was not within OWL DL, but is permissible in OWL 2 DL.

If the owl:disjointWith edges in the graph form
undirected complete subgraphs which share URIref nodes
but do not share blank node
then this may be within OWL DL.

A further example that cannot be generated from the mapping rule
for DisjointClasses.

If the owl:disjointWith edges in the graph form
undirected complete subgraphs which share URIref nodes
but do not share blank node
then this may be within OWL DL.
Two classes may be different names for the same set of individuals
Two classes may be different names for the same set of individuals
A version of WebOnt-equivalentClass-008 modified for the Direct Semantics, under which annotations in the entailed ontology are ignored.
A reasoner can also deduce that hasLeader is a subProperty of hasHead and hasHead is a subProperty of hasLeader.
The inverse entailment of test 002 also holds.
A Lite version of test imports-001.
dc:creator may be declared as an annotation property.
Annotation properties refer to a class instance. sameAs, in OWL Full, also refers to the class instance.
A role chain can be a synonym for transitivity.
The property hasAge is functional, but the individual a has two distinct hasAge fillers.
OWL 2 RL allows anonymous individual.
This test checks the interaction between an OWL functional property and differentFrom assertions.
This test checks the interaction between inverse functional property and differentFrom assertions.
A valid usage of oneOf in OWL 2 RL
Valid RL usage of allValuesFrom.
The range of hasAge is integer, but a has an asserted string hasAge filler.
